steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that "as renewable energy becomes more economically competitive, demand for petroleum falls." The correct choice must reflect this causal relationship: renewable energy competition drives down petroleum demand.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The demand for petroleum follows a predictable annual cycle.: describes a pattern not established in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — The competition from renewable energy is a factor in the demand for petroleum.: identifies renewable energy's growing competitiveness as a factor reducing petroleum demand — matches the passage's causal claim directly. ✓
  • Choice C — Companies involved in petroleum refining are not affected by the demand for petroleum.: denies that companies are affected, contradicting the passage's implication. ✗
  • Choice D — Companies involved in petroleum refining can benefit from a decline in the demand for petroleum.: reverses the relationship described in the passage. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
